After that I’ll synthesize the iteration report with clear wrapper-confirmed vs README-documented evidence and a tight “what this changes for us” angle.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8163:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne does not create a first-class session; it creates a collection context
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8167:- **Why it matters for us**: Mnemosyne’s lifecycle starts at “which collection am I in?”, while Public starts at “which trusted session is this?”. That is a materially weaker lifecycle model for resume, dedup, trust, and cleanup.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8171:### Finding 2: Mnemosyne session maintenance is agent-discipline, not runtime state
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8179:### Finding 3: Mnemosyne has no stop-hook summarization or autosave path
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8182:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s “end of session” behavior is only prompt guidance: store relevant memories before you finish. Public has a real stop path: extract a session summary from the last assistant message, persist it in hook state, and optionally call `generate-context.js` for autosave.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8183:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the biggest lifecycle gap. Mnemosyne can persist facts, but it does not natively summarize or checkpoint a session boundary. Public already does.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8187:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s compaction hook preserves memory awareness, not continuity state
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8190:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne injects a static checklist of memory verbs into `output.context` during compaction. Public’s compaction transport injects a rendered continuity note from a shared payload envelope and explicitly frames it as compaction continuity, separate from current-turn retrieval.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8191:- **Why it matters for us**: the transferable idea is not “compaction exists”; it is “memory-tool reminders survive compaction in plain prompt text.” Public’s substrate is stronger, but it currently emphasizes continuity payloads more than explicit memory-verb reminders.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8195:### Finding 5: Public has a trusted session boundary; Mnemosyne has none
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8203:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s lifecycle hygiene is document deletion, not session closure
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8231:- Questions addressed: implicit versus explicit session creation, session maintenance state, stop-time summarization, compaction survival, trusted session identity, stale-session cleanup, Public hybrid retrieval overlap
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8232:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne has no first-class session object; continuity is mostly prompt discipline plus collection scoping; compaction preserves tool awareness but not live continuity state; Public already has server-minted session IDs, working-memory continuity, cached summary validation, session-stop autosave, and stale cleanup; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` despite the shell warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`

Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:64:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:72:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:78:| 001 | Engram | MCP memory server (Go, SQLite+FTS5) | 004 (FTS5 search) | Focus tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic keys |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:85:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:87:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.

Examine the `experimental.session.compacting` hook implementation in `src/index.ts` line by line. Treat this as the most novel feature of the phase: capture exactly what guidance is injected, when it runs, and how that compares with Public's structural compaction plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:9:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:17:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:23:| 001 | Engram | MCP memory server (Go, SQLite+FTS5) | 004 (FTS5 search) | Focus tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic keys |

It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:32:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.

Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:4938:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:4946:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:4952:| 001 | Engram | MCP memory server (Go, SQLite+FTS5) | 004 (FTS5 search) | Focus tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic keys |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:4959:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:4961:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5043:- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5929:### Finding 1: The external repo is a deliberately thin wrapper, not a full memory engine
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5932:- **What it does**: the whole plugin surface lives in one entrypoint: OpenCode loads the plugin, resolves `ctx.directory || ctx.worktree || process.cwd()`, derives a project key, shells out to `mnemosyne`, registers five tools, and adds one compaction hook. `package.json` publishes only `dist`, and there is no in-repo AGENTS file or deeper architecture layer to inspect.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5933:- **Why it matters for us**: the transfer value is wrapper ergonomics and lifecycle design, not hidden TypeScript retrieval logic. We should treat backend claims as an interface contract, not as code we can adopt line-for-line.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5937:### Finding 2: Project/global scoping is the main product decision in the wrapper
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5940:- **What it does**: project scope is derived from the current directory basename, normalized so literal `global` becomes `default`; project collections are auto-initialized on plugin load, while the global collection is lazily initialized only on first `memory_store_global`. The tool surface makes project/global choice explicit with separate recall/store variants.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5945:### Finding 3: The CLI bridge is secure-by-construction, but shifts complexity into binary/runtime operations
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5949:- **Why it matters for us**: this is a clean plugin boundary if we ever want a standalone memory binary, but it trades in-process native-module complexity for external install, model bootstrap, and support burden.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5953:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s hybrid retrieval story is mostly README contract; Public already implements the deeper live pipeline
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5957:- **Why it matters for us**: we should reject any conclusion that Mnemosyne introduces hybrid retrieval as a net-new capability for Public. The real novelty is wrapper shape, scoping ergonomics, and compaction guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5961:### Finding 5: The compaction hook is the strongest transferable idea because it preserves tool awareness, not just context payload
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5964:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne pushes a static instruction block into `output.context` during `experimental.session.compacting`, explicitly naming `memory_recall`, `memory_store`, `memory_delete`, and the global variants. Public already preserves structural context through the compact-code-graph transport and can prepend surfaced constitutional/triggered memories during compaction, but that path does not add the same blunt “you still have these memory tools, and here is when to use them” reminder.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5965:- **Why it matters for us**: this is a low-cost, high-leverage addition for compaction resilience. Structural continuity and surfaced memories help, but direct tool reminders reduce the chance that the agent forgets to recall/store/delete after a context reset.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5969:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s AGENTS guidance is reusable, but CocoIndex should remain a separate system
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5972:- **What it does**: the README recommends a concrete operating rhythm: recall at session start, store after meaningful decisions, delete contradicted memories, use global variants for cross-project facts, and reserve core for always-needed context. CocoIndex, meanwhile, is explicitly positioned as semantic code search, with separate lifecycle and routing rules from memory retrieval.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5973:- **Why it matters for us**: the discipline pattern is worth adopting in Public prompts and compaction guidance, but merging memory retrieval with semantic code search would blur two distinct object types: human/project memory versus code discovery.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5992:- Questions addressed: hybrid retrieval boundary, model/runtime implications, project-vs-global scoping, compaction survival, AGENTS guidance, CocoIndex separation, plugin-vs-MCP adoption shape, offline-first tradeoffs
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5993:- Questions answered: the wrapper is thin; hybrid ranking is README-level not wrapper-owned; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval; project/global split is useful but collision-prone; compaction tool reminders are the best adopt-now idea; CocoIndex should stay separate from memory retrieval
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5998:- What worked: reading in the mandated order exposed the true architecture quickly; comparing wrapper code against Public’s live hybrid and compaction code prevented stale conclusions
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5999:- What did not work: the phase prompt’s external path included an extra `opencode-mnemosyne-main` segment that does not exist on disk; the filesystem is read-only here, so I could not create `research/research.md`, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6002:Investigate lifecycle ergonomics in more detail: document ID flow from recall to delete, basename-collision risks for project scoping, whether Public should add a dedicated memory-tool compaction reminder block, and whether an optional OpenCode memory plugin facade is worth prototyping on top of the existing MCP-backed memory system.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6016:### Finding 1: The external repo is a deliberately thin wrapper, not a full memory engine
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6019:- **What it does**: the whole plugin surface lives in one entrypoint: OpenCode loads the plugin, resolves `ctx.directory || ctx.worktree || process.cwd()`, derives a project key, shells out to `mnemosyne`, registers five tools, and adds one compaction hook. `package.json` publishes only `dist`, and there is no in-repo AGENTS file or deeper architecture layer to inspect.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6020:- **Why it matters for us**: the transfer value is wrapper ergonomics and lifecycle design, not hidden TypeScript retrieval logic. We should treat backend claims as an interface contract, not as code we can adopt line-for-line.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6024:### Finding 2: Project/global scoping is the main product decision in the wrapper
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6027:- **What it does**: project scope is derived from the current directory basename, normalized so literal `global` becomes `default`; project collections are auto-initialized on plugin load, while the global collection is lazily initialized only on first `memory_store_global`. The tool surface makes project/global choice explicit with separate recall/store variants.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6032:### Finding 3: The CLI bridge is secure-by-construction, but shifts complexity into binary/runtime operations
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6036:- **Why it matters for us**: this is a clean plugin boundary if we ever want a standalone memory binary, but it trades in-process native-module complexity for external install, model bootstrap, and support burden.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6040:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s hybrid retrieval story is mostly README contract; Public already implements the deeper live pipeline
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6044:- **Why it matters for us**: we should reject any conclusion that Mnemosyne introduces hybrid retrieval as a net-new capability for Public. The real novelty is wrapper shape, scoping ergonomics, and compaction guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6048:### Finding 5: The compaction hook is the strongest transferable idea because it preserves tool awareness, not just context payload
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6051:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne pushes a static instruction block into `output.context` during `experimental.session.compacting`, explicitly naming `memory_recall`, `memory_store`, `memory_delete`, and the global variants. Public already preserves structural context through the compact-code-graph transport and can prepend surfaced constitutional/triggered memories during compaction, but that path does not add the same blunt “you still have these memory tools, and here is when to use them” reminder.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6052:- **Why it matters for us**: this is a low-cost, high-leverage addition for compaction resilience. Structural continuity and surfaced memories help, but direct tool reminders reduce the chance that the agent forgets to recall/store/delete after a context reset.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6056:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s AGENTS guidance is reusable, but CocoIndex should remain a separate system
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6059:- **What it does**: the README recommends a concrete operating rhythm: recall at session start, store after meaningful decisions, delete contradicted memories, use global variants for cross-project facts, and reserve core for always-needed context. CocoIndex, meanwhile, is explicitly positioned as semantic code search, with separate lifecycle and routing rules from memory retrieval.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6060:- **Why it matters for us**: the discipline pattern is worth adopting in Public prompts and compaction guidance, but merging memory retrieval with semantic code search would blur two distinct object types: human/project memory versus code discovery.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6079:- Questions addressed: hybrid retrieval boundary, model/runtime implications, project-vs-global scoping, compaction survival, AGENTS guidance, CocoIndex separation, plugin-vs-MCP adoption shape, offline-first tradeoffs
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6080:- Questions answered: the wrapper is thin; hybrid ranking is README-level not wrapper-owned; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval; project/global split is useful but collision-prone; compaction tool reminders are the best adopt-now idea; CocoIndex should stay separate from memory retrieval
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6085:- What worked: reading in the mandated order exposed the true architecture quickly; comparing wrapper code against Public’s live hybrid and compaction code prevented stale conclusions
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6086:- What did not work: the phase prompt’s external path included an extra `opencode-mnemosyne-main` segment that does not exist on disk; the filesystem is read-only here, so I could not create `research/research.md`, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:6089:Investigate lifecycle ergonomics in more detail: document ID flow from recall to delete, basename-collision risks for project scoping, whether Public should add a dedicated memory-tool compaction reminder block, and whether an optional OpenCode memory plugin facade is worth prototyping on top of the existing MCP-backed memory system.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:9:### Finding 3: The documented hybrid stack is conventional and useful, but only README-proven in this repo
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:16:### Finding 4: Public already exceeds Mnemosyne’s hybrid retrieval depth by routing and fusing more channels before and after retrieval
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:23:### Finding 5: Public’s lexical and fallback behavior is materially richer than Mnemosyne’s visible surface
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:30:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s genuinely new search-facing idea is simpler scoped tool ergonomics, not better ranking
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:32:- **What it does**: project scope is derived from the current directory basename, project memory is auto-initialized on plugin load, global memory is lazily initialized on first write, and the tool surface makes project/global recall feel explicit and lightweight.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:57:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper proves only a thin search bridge; its hybrid ranking claims are README-level; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval with query routing, lexical weighting, adaptive fusion, and fallback tiers; the most transferable search idea is simpler project/global memory tool ergonomics, not the backend retrieval stack; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:71:You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:75:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:83:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:89:| 001 | Engram | MCP memory server (Go, SQLite+FTS5) | 004 (FTS5 search) | Focus tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic keys |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:96:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:98:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.

'call session_bootstrap()' :
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5714:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne treats contradiction cleanup as agent behavior, not as a first-class revision model
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5716:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, Mnemosyne exposes `memory_delete(id)` and injects compaction guidance telling the agent to delete contradicted memories before storing new ones; README guidance says the same. There is no wrapper-visible `update`, `supersede`, lineage, or revision table. Public instead deprecates predecessors on save, records lineage transitions, and appends history entries.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5721:### Finding 2: Mnemosyne has no wrapper-level dedup or upsert path; every store is a blind append
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5728:### Finding 3: Mnemosyne’s topic key stability depends on directory basename, which is fragile across renames and sibling worktrees
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5735:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s deletion flow is hard-delete by opaque numeric ID from plain-text recall output
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5742:### Finding 5: The compaction hook is Mnemosyne’s best hygiene idea, but it is guidance-only
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5744:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, Mnemosyne injects a compact “Persistent Memory” block during `experimental.session.compacting` that reminds the agent when to recall, store, delete contradicted entries, use `core`, and use global scope. Public’s compaction plugin injects transport content blocks, while server instructions advertise key tools but do not inject the same direct memory-discipline checklist into compaction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5749:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s wrapper shows no garbage collection or soft-delete lifecycle, while Public already has both
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5751:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, Mnemosyne’s visible lifecycle is `init`, `search`, `add`, and `delete`; there is no wrapper-level archive, unarchive, expiry, or sweep behavior. Public persists `retention_policy`, `delete_after`, and `expires_at`, excludes expired or archived rows at query time, and runs archive/unarchive plus background archival scans.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5752:- **Why it matters for us**: For hygiene, Mnemosyne is not a lifecycle-management upgrade over Public. If we ever front an external binary, we would need an adapter that preserves Public’s retention and soft-delete semantics.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5780:- Questions addressed: dedup/upsert behavior, revision tracking, delete semantics, garbage collection, soft deletes, topic key stability, compaction hygiene guidance, auditability of mutation flows
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5781:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5785:- What did not work: the external repo is intentionally minimal, so many lifecycle questions stop at wrapper absence rather than deeper source proof; this sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js` to save phase memory
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5801:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne treats contradiction cleanup as agent behavior, not as a first-class revision model

There is no wrapper-visible `update`, `supersede`, lineage, or revision table. Public instead deprecates predecessors on save, records lineage transitions, and appends history entries.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5868:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5872:- What did not work: the external repo is intentionally minimal, so many lifecycle questions stop at wrapper absence rather than deeper source proof; this sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js` to save phase memory
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:1:### Finding 4: The project-first / global-lazy split is a useful ergonomic pattern, even though the scope model is too weak
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:3:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, the project collection is auto-initialized on plugin load, while the global collection is initialized only on first global write. That asymmetry makes project memory frictionless and global memory explicit. Public already supports broader local/global retrieval semantics through `retrievalLevel: "local" | "global" | "auto"`, but not via a similarly simple project/global tool pair.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:8:### Finding 5: Mnemosyne’s strongest agent-integration idea is a repeated memory-discipline checklist across AGENTS and compaction
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:10:- **What it does**: Both wrapper-confirmed and README-documented, the same behavioral rules show up twice: search when history matters, store concise decisions, delete contradictions, reserve `core` for always-needed facts, and use global only for cross-project preferences. Public already injects startup and compaction notes, but those notes are more about recovery and structural context than a short memory-discipline checklist.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:15:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s hybrid search story stays behind the backend contract, so Public should keep memory retrieval and semantic code search separate
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:36:- [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts)
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:40:- Questions addressed: agent-agnostic plugin shape, passive capture vs manual capture, multi-agent safety, project/global scoping ergonomics, AGENTS guidance, compaction survival, hybrid-search boundary, CocoIndex separation, offline/local integration assumptions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:41:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper is intentionally minimal and portable, but it delegates nearly all safe behavior to agent discipline; it has no passive capture or actor-scoped governance, its best transferable idea is the repeated memory-discipline checklist across AGENTS and compaction, and its project-first/global-lazy ergonomics are useful even though the underlying scope model is too weak for Public.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:46:- What did not work: the external repo is so thin that several deeper questions terminate at wrapper absence rather than richer source proof; this sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js` to save memory.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:49:Trace the adapter contract for an external-binary memory facade that preserves Public-grade multi-agent safety: required structured response envelope, actor/session scope propagation, project-first/global escalation rules, mutation confirmation semantics, and a shared memory-discipline checklist injected at both startup and compaction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:56:You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:60:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:68:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:74:| 001 | Engram | MCP memory server (Go, SQLite+FTS5) | 004 (FTS5 search) | Focus tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic keys |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:81:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:83:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.

Public’s compaction and bootstrap surfaces already inject runtime-specific structural status and route code-search questions toward CocoIndex or `code_graph_query`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4526:### Finding 6: The plugin package is too thin to treat Mnemosyne as a ranking reference implementation

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4535:- **What it does**: Missing binary handling returns a plain error string, project init silently swallows failures, and the README documents a first-run model download of about 500 MB. Public exposes explicit bootstrap, health, graph, and code-search readiness surfaces instead of making retrieval availability implicit.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4547:- [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts)

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4563:- What did not work: the package is intentionally too thin to prove backend ranking behavior beyond the README contract, and the read-only sandbox prevented updating `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or saving memory via `generate-context.js`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4566:Define an external-binary adapter contract for Public that preserves structured retrieval envelopes, trusted session propagation, readiness/health signaling, and optional project/global UX aliases without collapsing `memory_search`, CocoIndex, and code-graph into a single opaque recall surface.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4614:### Finding 6: The plugin package is too thin to treat Mnemosyne as a ranking reference implementation

Public exposes explicit bootstrap, health, graph, and code-search readiness surfaces instead of making retrieval availability implicit.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4635:- [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts)
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4646:- Questions addressed: wrapper-visible hybrid-search contract, query handoff opacity, retrieval scope ergonomics, session-aware continuity, compaction-time retrieval guidance, package/audit boundary, offline readiness and failure handling.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4651:- What did not work: the package is intentionally too thin to prove backend ranking behavior beyond the README contract, and the read-only sandbox prevented updating `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or saving memory via `generate-context.js`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4654:Define an external-binary adapter contract for Public that preserves structured retrieval envelopes, trusted session propagation, readiness/health signaling, and optional project/global UX aliases without collapsing `memory_search`, CocoIndex, and code-graph into a single opaque recall surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:1:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s deletion flow is hard-delete by opaque numeric ID from plain-text recall output
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:8:### Finding 5: The compaction hook is Mnemosyne’s best hygiene idea, but it is guidance-only
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, Mnemosyne injects a compact “Persistent Memory” block during `experimental.session.compacting` that reminds the agent when to recall, store, delete contradicted entries, use `core`, and use global scope. Public’s compaction plugin injects transport content blocks, while server instructions advertise key tools but do not inject the same direct memory-discipline checklist into compaction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:15:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s wrapper shows no garbage collection or soft-delete lifecycle, while Public already has both
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:17:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, Mnemosyne’s visible lifecycle is `init`, `search`, `add`, and `delete`; there is no wrapper-level archive, unarchive, expiry, or sweep behavior. Public persists `retention_policy`, `delete_after`, and `expires_at`, excludes expired or archived rows at query time, and runs archive/unarchive plus background archival scans.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:18:- **Why it matters for us**: For hygiene, Mnemosyne is not a lifecycle-management upgrade over Public. If we ever front an external binary, we would need an adapter that preserves Public’s retention and soft-delete semantics.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:46:- Questions addressed: dedup/upsert behavior, revision tracking, delete semantics, garbage collection, soft deletes, topic key stability, compaction hygiene guidance, auditability of mutation flows
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:47:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:51:- What did not work: the external repo is intentionally minimal, so many lifecycle questions stop at wrapper absence rather than deeper source proof; this sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js` to save phase memory
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:61:You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:65:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:73:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:79:| 001 | Engram | MCP memory server (Go, SQLite+FTS5) | 004 (FTS5 search) | Focus tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic keys |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:86:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:88:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
