I’m doing one last pass over the external folder structure and a few targeted Public files so the final findings stay anchored on agent integration rather than drifting back into generic retrieval or lifecycle comparisons.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5002:   208	    // Inject memory instructions into compaction so they survive
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5051:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne is an intentionally thin, agent-agnostic OpenCode wrapper
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5053:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, the repo is basically one plugin entrypoint plus package metadata: it resolves `directory || worktree || cwd`, shells out through `Bun.spawn(["mnemosyne", ...args])`, and returns five tools plus one compaction hook. There is no runtime-specific orchestration layer, no extra policy engine, and no structured adapter boundary beyond raw CLI calls.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5058:### Finding 2: Mnemosyne has no passive capture path; it is manual-store plus reminder-driven behavior
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5060:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, storing only happens when the agent explicitly calls `memory_store` or `memory_store_global`; the plugin itself never auto-captures decisions. The only passive behavior is reminder text in the compaction hook, reinforced by README-recommended `AGENTS.md` instructions. Public already goes further with session priming plus auto-surfacing at tool dispatch and compaction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5065:### Finding 3: Multi-agent safety is effectively absent at the plugin boundary
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5072:### Finding 4: The project-first / global-lazy split is a useful ergonomic pattern, even though the scope model is too weak
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5074:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, the project collection is auto-initialized on plugin load, while the global collection is initialized only on first global write. That asymmetry makes project memory frictionless and global memory explicit. Public already supports broader local/global retrieval semantics through `retrievalLevel: "local" | "global" | "auto"`, but not via a similarly simple project/global tool pair.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5079:### Finding 5: Mnemosyne’s strongest agent-integration idea is a repeated memory-discipline checklist across AGENTS and compaction
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5081:- **What it does**: Both wrapper-confirmed and README-documented, the same behavioral rules show up twice: search when history matters, store concise decisions, delete contradictions, reserve `core` for always-needed facts, and use global only for cross-project preferences. Public already injects startup and compaction notes, but those notes are more about recovery and structural context than a short memory-discipline checklist.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5086:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s hybrid search story stays behind the backend contract, so Public should keep memory retrieval and semantic code search separate
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5107:- [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts)
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5111:- Questions addressed: agent-agnostic plugin shape, passive capture vs manual capture, multi-agent safety, project/global scoping ergonomics, AGENTS guidance, compaction survival, hybrid-search boundary, CocoIndex separation, offline/local integration assumptions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5112:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper is intentionally minimal and portable, but it delegates nearly all safe behavior to agent discipline; it has no passive capture or actor-scoped governance, its best transferable idea is the repeated memory-discipline checklist across AGENTS and compaction, and its project-first/global-lazy ergonomics are useful even though the underlying scope model is too weak for Public.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5117:- What did not work: the external repo is so thin that several deeper questions terminate at wrapper absence rather than richer source proof; this sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js` to save memory.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5120:Trace the adapter contract for an external-binary memory facade that preserves Public-grade multi-agent safety: required structured response envelope, actor/session scope propagation, project-first/global escalation rules, mutation confirmation semantics, and a shared memory-discipline checklist injected at both startup and compaction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5133:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne is an intentionally thin, agent-agnostic OpenCode wrapper
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5135:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, the repo is basically one plugin entrypoint plus package metadata: it resolves `directory || worktree || cwd`, shells out through `Bun.spawn(["mnemosyne", ...args])`, and returns five tools plus one compaction hook. There is no runtime-specific orchestration layer, no extra policy engine, and no structured adapter boundary beyond raw CLI calls.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5140:### Finding 2: Mnemosyne has no passive capture path; it is manual-store plus reminder-driven behavior
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5142:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, storing only happens when the agent explicitly calls `memory_store` or `memory_store_global`; the plugin itself never auto-captures decisions. The only passive behavior is reminder text in the compaction hook, reinforced by README-recommended `AGENTS.md` instructions. Public already goes further with session priming plus auto-surfacing at tool dispatch and compaction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5147:### Finding 3: Multi-agent safety is effectively absent at the plugin boundary
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5154:### Finding 4: The project-first / global-lazy split is a useful ergonomic pattern, even though the scope model is too weak
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5156:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, the project collection is auto-initialized on plugin load, while the global collection is initialized only on first global write. That asymmetry makes project memory frictionless and global memory explicit. Public already supports broader local/global retrieval semantics through `retrievalLevel: "local" | "global" | "auto"`, but not via a similarly simple project/global tool pair.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5189:- [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts)

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5199:- What did not work: the external repo is so thin that several deeper questions terminate at wrapper absence rather than richer source proof; this sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js` to save memory.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5202:Trace the adapter contract for an external-binary memory facade that preserves Public-grade multi-agent safety: required structured response envelope, actor/session scope propagation, project-first/global escalation rules, mutation confirmation semantics, and a shared memory-discipline checklist injected at both startup and compaction.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:1:### Finding 3: The CLI bridge is secure-by-construction, but shifts complexity into binary/runtime operations
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:5:- **Why it matters for us**: this is a clean plugin boundary if we ever want a standalone memory binary, but it trades in-process native-module complexity for external install, model bootstrap, and support burden.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:9:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s hybrid retrieval story is mostly README contract; Public already implements the deeper live pipeline
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:13:- **Why it matters for us**: we should reject any conclusion that Mnemosyne introduces hybrid retrieval as a net-new capability for Public. The real novelty is wrapper shape, scoping ergonomics, and compaction guidance.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:17:### Finding 5: The compaction hook is the strongest transferable idea because it preserves tool awareness, not just context payload
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:20:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne pushes a static instruction block into `output.context` during `experimental.session.compacting`, explicitly naming `memory_recall`, `memory_store`, `memory_delete`, and the global variants. Public already preserves structural context through the compact-code-graph transport and can prepend surfaced constitutional/triggered memories during compaction, but that path does not add the same blunt “you still have these memory tools, and here is when to use them” reminder.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:21:- **Why it matters for us**: this is a low-cost, high-leverage addition for compaction resilience. Structural continuity and surfaced memories help, but direct tool reminders reduce the chance that the agent forgets to recall/store/delete after a context reset.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:25:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s AGENTS guidance is reusable, but CocoIndex should remain a separate system
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:28:- **What it does**: the README recommends a concrete operating rhythm: recall at session start, store after meaningful decisions, delete contradicted memories, use global variants for cross-project facts, and reserve core for always-needed context. CocoIndex, meanwhile, is explicitly positioned as semantic code search, with separate lifecycle and routing rules from memory retrieval.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:29:- **Why it matters for us**: the discipline pattern is worth adopting in Public prompts and compaction guidance, but merging memory retrieval with semantic code search would blur two distinct object types: human/project memory versus code discovery.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:48:- Questions addressed: hybrid retrieval boundary, model/runtime implications, project-vs-global scoping, compaction survival, AGENTS guidance, CocoIndex separation, plugin-vs-MCP adoption shape, offline-first tradeoffs
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:49:- Questions answered: the wrapper is thin; hybrid ranking is README-level not wrapper-owned; Public already has deeper hybrid retrieval; project/global split is useful but collision-prone; compaction tool reminders are the best adopt-now idea; CocoIndex should stay separate from memory retrieval
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:54:- What worked: reading in the mandated order exposed the true architecture quickly; comparing wrapper code against Public’s live hybrid and compaction code prevented stale conclusions
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:55:- What did not work: the phase prompt’s external path included an extra `opencode-mnemosyne-main` segment that does not exist on disk; the filesystem is read-only here, so I could not create `research/research.md`, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:58:Investigate lifecycle ergonomics in more detail: document ID flow from recall to delete, basename-collision risks for project scoping, whether Public should add a dedicated memory-tool compaction reminder block, and whether an optional OpenCode memory plugin facade is worth prototyping on top of the existing MCP-backed memory system.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:65:You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:69:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:77:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:83:| 001 | Engram | MCP memory server (Go, SQLite+FTS5) | 004 (FTS5 search) | Focus tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic keys |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:90:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:92:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:102:7. Examine `memory_delete` in detail. Trace how document IDs are expected to flow from recall results into deletion, and what this says about memory lifecycle and hygiene expectations.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:103:8. Examine the `experimental.session.compacting` hook implementation in `src/index.ts` line by line. Treat this as the most novel feature of the phase: capture exactly what guidance is injected, when it runs, and how that compares with Public's structural compaction plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:104:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:106:11. Compare Mnemosyne directly against current `Code_Environment/Public` code: `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts` for existing hybrid retrieval, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts` for session bootstrap/resume behavior, `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` for compaction injection, and `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md` for semantic code-search positioning.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:107:12. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:114:    Research the external repository at /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/opencode-mnemosyne-main and identify concrete improvements for Code_Environment/Public, especially around hybrid search (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin architecture, memory scoping (project vs global), session compaction resilience, and offline-first memory design.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:126:4. What exactly does the compaction hook preserve, and how does prompt-level tool guidance differ from Public's current structural-context compaction transport?

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:19:### Finding 6: Extension-coupled vector storage raises operability requirements that Mnemosyne’s wrapper does not address
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:60:You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:64:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:72:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:78:| 001 | Engram | MCP memory server (Go, SQLite+FTS5) | 004 (FTS5 search) | Focus tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic keys |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:85:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:87:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:2894:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/research/research.md:280:- Phase 2 shifted the recommendation stack from additive feature work toward simplification and refactoring. Phase 3 sharpens that further: the next gains come from deleting front-door fragmentation while preserving the strong evidence layer underneath. Runtime-enforced gates, a cleaner memory split, workflow profiles, canonical lifecycle and continuity surfaces, smaller public skill sets, and generated operator guidance all reinforce each other. They are not independent tweaks. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:2899:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/research/research.md:312:Open the next implementation packet around one narrow proving ground: a canonical operator front door for one workflow family, ideally deep research or general lifecycle work. That prototype should combine four linked hypotheses at once: runtime-owned operator policy, one canonical lifecycle and continuity entry surface, conditional skill routing instead of mandatory Gate 2 ceremony, and generated harness-facing guidance from one machine-readable source. If that works, the repo will have a credible path into the bigger memory-split, workflow-profile, and iteration-engine refactors without changing everything at once. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:3391:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:27:The best import from the external repo is not "use exactly one command for everything." It is "make the operator choose the outcome, not the internal engine." `system-spec-kit` can keep separate internal workflows, but the public surface should be one primary lifecycle command with profiles like `new-work`, `continue-bound-work`, and `full-lifecycle`, rather than three sibling commands that each restate the machine room.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:3392:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:32:finding: `system-spec-kit` should merge the public `plan` / `implement` / `complete` lifecycle into one profile-driven entry surface, while keeping the current internal workflow separation behind that surface.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:3407:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/027-memory-plugin-and-refinement/001-memory-repo-analysis/scratch/legacy-memory-quarantine/17-12-25_14-00__roampal-analysis.md:149:Implemented the Memory Context Plugin for OpenCode (.opencode/plugin/memory-context.js). Used Sequential Thinking MCP for deep analysis of OpenCode plugin architecture. Discovered key hooks: experimental.chat.system.transform (for system prompt injection), chat.message (for trigger logging), and event handler (for session lifecycle). Plugin implements Layer 1 (constitutional context injection via system transform), Layer 2 (trigger matching with logging), and Layer 3 placeholder (exchange recording). Uses Bun's native bun:sqlite for zero dependencies. Plugin is 302 lines with caching, graceful degradation, and token budgeting (~500 tokens max for constitutional context).

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:3450:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/q-e-license-runtime.md:48:But two gates keep it out of `source-portable`. So the system is best scored `mixed`: many implementation ideas are technically reusable, but direct code adoption remains blocked today. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:3528:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/library/specializations/game-development/skills/unreal-development/SKILL.md:3:description: Unreal Engine integration skill for C++/Blueprint development, actor lifecycle management, plugin development, and editor automation. Enables LLMs to interact with Unreal Editor through MCP servers for level manipulation, Blueprint generation, and automated workflows.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:3645:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/deep-research-dashboard.md:63:2. Merge the public lifecycle and memory surfaces into a smaller profile-driven entry experience.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:3874:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/archive/research-v1-iter-8.md:30:Claudest finished as the most practically reusable system even though it was not the cleanest legal/runtime fit. Its key strength is continuity discipline: cached `context_summary` startup, deterministic Stop-time production, FTS capability detection, consolidation contracts, pricing/cache normalization, and explicit auditor-vs-discoverer roles all reinforce the owner that Public already calls Spec Kit Memory. Its biggest weakness is portability evidence rather than design quality: the snapshot lacks a canonical `LICENSE` file, the host model assumes Claude plugin lifecycles, and Public still lacks the producer-side fields needed to reproduce Claudest-grade analytics honestly. Public should still treat Claudest as the primary reference for the next implementation wave, because its best ideas compose inside one existing surface instead of pressuring the architecture toward a monolith. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6919:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne exposes a deliberately tiny plugin surface, not a full memory platform API
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6922:- **What it does**: the external repo returns one OpenCode plugin object with exactly five tools plus one compaction hook; there is no HTTP surface, no MCP server, and no secondary registration layer. By contrast, Public’s memory server test suite locks `TOOL_DEFINITIONS.length` at 43.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6927:### Finding 2: The CLI bridge is safely implemented, but operationally under-instrumented
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6931:- **Why it matters for us**: the argv-array bridge is worth copying if we ever front an external memory binary, but the missing operational tools would be a regression versus Public’s `ccc_status`, `session_bootstrap`, and richer MCP diagnostics.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6935:### Finding 3: Mnemosyne’s project/global split is ergonomic, but the actual scope key is weak
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6943:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne has no profile separation; Public does
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6946:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne offers one fixed tool family and one fixed compaction prompt block. Public supports response `profile` values on `memory_context`, intent-to-profile auto-routing, and runtime-level profile separation in `.codex/config.toml` (`context`, `research`, `review`, `write`, etc.).
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6951:### Finding 5: The compaction hook is the most transferable idea, but Public’s implementation substrate is stronger
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6954:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne injects a static memory-usage checklist into `experimental.session.compacting`. Public’s compact-code-graph plugin already does compaction injection too, but through a cached bridge transport plan, with dedupe checks and a plugin status tool.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6955:- **Why it matters for us**: the novel reusable part is not “compaction hook exists” but “memory-tool reminders survive compaction directly in prompt text.” Public can add that guidance inside its existing transport-plan architecture instead of copying Mnemosyne’s thinner standalone pattern.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6959:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s hybrid retrieval and offline-first story are mostly backend contract, not wrapper logic
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6982:- Questions addressed: tool registration, CLI bridge semantics, search/store/delete verbs, project vs global scoping, compaction prompt injection, profile separation, offline-first operational surface, Public overlap boundaries
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6983:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo proves a thin OpenCode plugin facade over a CLI binary; the real hybrid-search mechanics remain README-level; Public already exceeds it on MCP breadth, layered routing, and profile-aware recovery; the strongest reusable idea is direct memory-tool guidance during compaction, not the backend contract itself; the strict validation command was run and reported `RESULT: PASSED` despite a read-only temp-file warning from the validator shell
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:6986:- What worked: reading `src/index.ts` first made the boundary obvious very quickly, and comparing it directly with Public’s tool schemas and compaction plugin prevented stale “Public lacks hybrid retrieval” conclusions
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:7003:### Finding 1: Mnemosyne exposes a deliberately tiny plugin surface, not a full memory platform API

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5751:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, Mnemosyne’s visible lifecycle is `init`, `search`, `add`, and `delete`; there is no wrapper-level archive, unarchive, expiry, or sweep behavior. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5752:- **Why it matters for us**: For hygiene, Mnemosyne is not a lifecycle-management upgrade over Public. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5781:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4654:Define an external-binary adapter contract for Public that preserves structured retrieval envelopes, trusted session propagation, readiness/health signaling, and optional project/global UX aliases without collapsing `memory_search`, CocoIndex, and code-graph into a single opaque recall surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:9:### Finding 5: Public has a trusted session boundary; Mnemosyne has none
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:12:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne tools accept only query/content/id arguments; there is no trusted session handoff or identity binding. Public validates caller-supplied `sessionId`, rejects untrusted IDs, and instructs callers to omit it so the server can mint a new trusted session.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:17:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s lifecycle hygiene is document deletion, not session closure
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:45:- Questions addressed: implicit versus explicit session creation, session maintenance state, stop-time summarization, compaction survival, trusted session identity, stale-session cleanup, Public hybrid retrieval overlap

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:50:- What did not work: the brief assumed `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/...`, but the checkout is actually rooted directly under `external/`; the sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js` to save memory for this phase

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:17:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, Mnemosyne’s visible lifecycle is `init`, `search`, `add`, and `delete`; there is no wrapper-level archive, unarchive, expiry, or sweep behavior. Public persists `retention_policy`, `delete_after`, and `expires_at`, excludes expired or archived rows at query time, and runs archive/unarchive plus background archival scans.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:18:- **Why it matters for us**: For hygiene, Mnemosyne is not a lifecycle-management upgrade over Public. If we ever front an external binary, we would need an adapter that preserves Public’s retention and soft-delete semantics.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:47:- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s wrapper exposes no dedup, no upsert, no revision lineage, no soft-delete lifecycle, and no retention/GC contract; contradiction hygiene is primarily agent guidance plus hard delete; basename-derived project scoping is materially less stable than Public’s canonical-path plus logical-key approach; the compaction reminder block is the only clear hygiene improvement worth borrowing immediately; the exact strict validation command returned `RESULT: PASSED` with the warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`

After that I’ll synthesize the iteration report with clear wrapper-confirmed vs README-documented evidence and a tight “what this changes for us” angle.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8167:- **Why it matters for us**: Mnemosyne’s lifecycle starts at “which collection am I in?”, while Public starts at “which trusted session is this?”. That is a materially weaker lifecycle model for resume, dedup, trust, and cleanup.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8174:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne exposes stateless recall/store/delete tools and recommends that the agent use them at session start and during decision points. Public keeps explicit retrieval session state: seen-result dedup, active goal, preferred anchors, inferred mode, event counters, and auto-resume prompt context injection.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8182:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s “end of session” behavior is only prompt guidance: store relevant memories before you finish. Public has a real stop path: extract a session summary from the last assistant message, persist it in hook state, and optionally call `generate-context.js` for autosave.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8183:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the biggest lifecycle gap. Mnemosyne can persist facts, but it does not natively summarize or checkpoint a session boundary. Public already does.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8195:### Finding 5: Public has a trusted session boundary; Mnemosyne has none
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8198:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne tools accept only query/content/id arguments; there is no trusted session handoff or identity binding. Public validates caller-supplied `sessionId`, rejects untrusted IDs, and instructs callers to omit it so the server can mint a new trusted session.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8203:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s lifecycle hygiene is document deletion, not session closure
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8231:- Questions addressed: implicit versus explicit session creation, session maintenance state, stop-time summarization, compaction survival, trusted session identity, stale-session cleanup, Public hybrid retrieval overlap
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8235:- What worked: starting with `external/src/index.ts` made it obvious that the lifecycle logic lives almost entirely in the wrapper boundary and prompt contract; tracing that directly into Public’s `session-manager`, `session-resume`, `memory-context`, and `session-stop` code exposed the real lifecycle delta fast
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8236:- What did not work: the brief assumed `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/...`, but the checkout is actually rooted directly under `external/`; the sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js` to save memory for this phase
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8239:MUTATION + LINEAGE SAFETY: trace how Mnemosyne document IDs move from recall output into delete operations, compare that with Public’s checkpoint, session cleanup, and memory-save/reconsolidation boundaries, and define the minimum safe contract for any future external-binary memory facade: trusted session layer, close/cleanup surface, summary/autosave hook, and contradiction-safe mutation path.

That is a materially weaker lifecycle model for resume, dedup, trust, and cleanup.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8261:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne exposes stateless recall/store/delete tools and recommends that the agent use them at session start and during decision points. Public keeps explicit retrieval session state: seen-result dedup, active goal, preferred anchors, inferred mode, event counters, and auto-resume prompt context injection.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8269:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s “end of session” behavior is only prompt guidance: store relevant memories before you finish. Public has a real stop path: extract a session summary from the last assistant message, persist it in hook state, and optionally call `generate-context.js` for autosave.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8270:- **Why it matters for us**: this is the biggest lifecycle gap. Mnemosyne can persist facts, but it does not natively summarize or checkpoint a session boundary. Public already does.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8282:### Finding 5: Public has a trusted session boundary; Mnemosyne has none
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8285:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne tools accept only query/content/id arguments; there is no trusted session handoff or identity binding. Public validates caller-supplied `sessionId`, rejects untrusted IDs, and instructs callers to omit it so the server can mint a new trusted session.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8290:### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s lifecycle hygiene is document deletion, not session closure
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8318:- Questions addressed: implicit versus explicit session creation, session maintenance state, stop-time summarization, compaction survival, trusted session identity, stale-session cleanup, Public hybrid retrieval overlap
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8322:- What worked: starting with `external/src/index.ts` made it obvious that the lifecycle logic lives almost entirely in the wrapper boundary and prompt contract; tracing that directly into Public’s `session-manager`, `session-resume`, `memory-context`, and `session-stop` code exposed the real lifecycle delta fast
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8323:- What did not work: the brief assumed `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/...`, but the checkout is actually rooted directly under `external/`; the sandbox is read-only, so I could not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js` to save memory for this phase
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8326:MUTATION + LINEAGE SAFETY: trace how Mnemosyne document IDs move from recall output into delete operations, compare that with Public’s checkpoint, session cleanup, and memory-save/reconsolidation boundaries, and define the minimum safe contract for any future external-binary memory facade: trusted session layer, close/cleanup surface, summary/autosave hook, and contradiction-safe mutation path.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:5:- **Why it matters for us**: this is a clean plugin boundary if we ever want a standalone memory binary, but it trades in-process native-module complexity for external install, model bootstrap, and support burden.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:28:- **What it does**: the README recommends a concrete operating rhythm: recall at session start, store after meaningful decisions, delete contradicted memories, use global variants for cross-project facts, and reserve core for always-needed context. CocoIndex, meanwhile, is explicitly positioned as semantic code search, with separate lifecycle and routing rules from memory retrieval.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:55:- What did not work: the phase prompt’s external path included an extra `opencode-mnemosyne-main` segment that does not exist on disk; the filesystem is read-only here, so I could not create `research/research.md`, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js`

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:2894:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/research/research.md:280:- Phase 2 shifted the recommendation stack from additive feature work toward simplification and refactoring. Phase 3 sharpens that further: the next gains come from deleting front-door fragmentation while preserving the strong evidence layer underneath. Runtime-enforced gates, a cleaner memory split, workflow profiles, canonical lifecycle and continuity surfaces, smaller public skill sets, and generated operator guidance all reinforce each other. They are not independent tweaks. [SOURCE: external/library/methodologies/spec-kit/spec-kit-orchestrator.js:114-170] [SOURCE: .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/memory/generate-context.ts:75-124] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/CLAUDE.md:237-242]

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:2899:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/research/research.md:312:Open the next implementation packet around one narrow proving ground: a canonical operator front door for one workflow family, ideally deep research or general lifecycle work. That prototype should combine four linked hypotheses at once: runtime-owned operator policy, one canonical lifecycle and continuity entry surface, conditional skill routing instead of mandatory Gate 2 ceremony, and generated harness-facing guidance from one machine-readable source. If that works, the repo will have a credible path into the bigger memory-split, workflow-profile, and iteration-engine refactors without changing everything at once. [SOURCE: external/README.md:186-239] [SOURCE: external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/plugin.json:2-14] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/CLAUDE.md:237-242]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:3092:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:469:1. **Single-canonical-`ScanResult` orchestration shape.** Project one analysis result into multiple consumer surfaces (static markdown, MCP, profile files, blast-radius), branching late inside `main()` rather than running parallel pipelines. Anchor: `external/src/index.ts:75-175`. Public lift: define a shared `RepoFingerprint` (or similar) that any context-producing surface — CLAUDE.md generator, MCP tool, code-graph snapshot, spec-kit memory bootstrap — consumes from a single function. F-ORCH-1, F-ORCH-2.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:3391:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:27:The best import from the external repo is not "use exactly one command for everything." It is "make the operator choose the outcome, not the internal engine." `system-spec-kit` can keep separate internal workflows, but the public surface should be one primary lifecycle command with profiles like `new-work`, `continue-bound-work`, and `full-lifecycle`, rather than three sibling commands that each restate the machine room.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:3874:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/archive/research-v1-iter-8.md:30:Claudest finished as the most practically reusable system even though it was not the cleanest legal/runtime fit. Its key strength is continuity discipline: cached `context_summary` startup, deterministic Stop-time production, FTS capability detection, consolidation contracts, pricing/cache normalization, and explicit auditor-vs-discoverer roles all reinforce the owner that Public already calls Spec Kit Memory. Its biggest weakness is portability evidence rather than design quality: the snapshot lacks a canonical `LICENSE` file, the host model assumes Claude plugin lifecycles, and Public still lacks the producer-side fields needed to reproduce Claudest-grade analytics honestly. Public should still treat Claudest as the primary reference for the next implementation wave, because its best ideas compose inside one existing surface instead of pressuring the architecture toward a monolith. I checked the phase folder too: there are earlier iteration artifacts under `research/iterations/`, so I’m deliberately steering away from the already-covered wrapper-thinness and generic compaction points. This pass is narrowing to lifecycle ownership: who mints state, who verifies readiness, how context is resumed, and how session knowledge gets serialized for the next run.

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md | sed -n '40,90p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
    40	### What This Skill Does
    41	
    42	CocoIndex Code is a semantic code search tool built on vector embeddings. Where `grep` matches exact characters, CocoIndex Code matches meaning. Ask for "retry logic with exponential backoff" and it returns code that implements that pattern, regardless of how the author named variables or functions. This makes it the right tool when you know what a piece of code does but not where it lives or what it is called.
    43	
    44	The skill ships with two access modes. The CLI (`ccc`) is fastest for one-off queries and all index management operations. The MCP server (`ccc mcp`) exposes a single `search` tool that AI agents call directly via stdio transport, integrating semantic search into any tool-calling workflow without leaving the conversation.
    45	
    46	Indexing is incremental and daemon-backed. The first run scans and embeds all supported files in the project. Subsequent runs update only changed files. A background daemon starts automatically on the first command, persists across calls, and restarts itself when settings or the binary version change.
    47	
    48	### Key Statistics
    49	
    50	| Property | Value |
    51	|---|---|
    52	| Version | 1.0.0 |
    53	| MCP tools exposed | 1 (`search`) |
    54	| Supported languages | 28+ |
    55	| Default embedding model | `sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2` (local, no API key) |
    56	| Primary embedding model | `voyage/voyage-code-3` via LiteLLM (1024-dim, requires `VOYAGE_API_KEY`) |
    57	| Vector storage | SQLite via sqlite-vec |
    58	| Chunk size | 1000 chars, 250 char minimum, 150 char overlap |
    59	| Similarity metric | Cosine similarity (0.0 to 1.0) |
    60	
    61	### How This Compares
    62	
    63	| Tool | Use When | Limitation |
    64	|---|---|---|
    65	| `ccc search` (CocoIndex) | You know what code does but not where it lives | Approximate, needs verification |
    66	| `code_graph_query` | You need exact callers, imports, or structural dependencies | Requires the structural graph to be indexed first |
    67	| `Grep` | You know the exact text, symbol, or regex pattern | Cannot find conceptual matches |
    68	| `Glob` | You know the file name or extension pattern | Cannot search file contents |
    69	| `Read` | You know the exact file path | No search capability |
    70	
    71	### Key Features
    72	
    73	| Feature | Description |
    74	|---|---|
    75	| Semantic search | Query by concept or intent, not exact text |
    76	| CLI and MCP modes | `ccc` for terminal use, `ccc mcp` for AI agent integration |
    77	| Language filters | `--lang` (CLI) or `languages` (MCP) narrows results by language |
    78	| Path filters | `--path` (CLI) or `paths` (MCP) scopes results to a directory |
    79	| Incremental indexing | Only re-embeds changed files on subsequent runs |
    80	| Daemon architecture | Auto-starts, auto-restarts on version or settings change |
    81	| Spec Kit integration | Companion lifecycle tools (`ccc_status`, `ccc_reindex`, `ccc_feedback`) and code-graph/session integration are available through system-spec-kit |
    82	| Two embedding models | Local (no API key) or cloud (higher quality) |
    83	| 28+ languages | Language-aware chunk splitting preserves function and class boundaries |
    84	
    85	In the broader system-spec-kit stack, CocoIndex is the semantic half of a three-system retrieval model: CocoIndex finds conceptually similar code, Code Graph answers structural questions, and session bootstrap surfaces CocoIndex readiness during recovery. The companion lifecycle helpers exposed through system-spec-kit are `ccc_status`, `ccc_reindex`, and `ccc_feedback`.
    86	
    87	<!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->
    88	
    89	---
    90
